title: Tricuspid valve annuloplasty ring
abstract: Tricuspid valve regurgitation is caused by the condition of annular dilation in majority of the patients. Tricuspid valve is a tri-leaflet structure with annular dilatation taking place mostly in anterior leaflet and posterior leaflet. Septal leaflet is usually spared. A Polytetrafluoroethyene PTFE fabric ring is created and once it is sutured to the annulus it reduces the size of the annulus and also reforms the shape of the annulus. This is a flexible ring it takes the shape of the annulus after suturing. The PTFE fabric ring has much of desirable properties of being able to get sutured continuous or interrupted sutures. The structural arrangement of the said PTFE material is uniform and exhibits homogeneity throughout and hence facilitating uniform distribution of stress throughout the material. Annuloplasty ring is made up of medically approved Polytetrafluoroethyene ( PTFE) material fabric which has long term durability properties.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is 1) Annuloplasty ring for surgical procedure of tricuspid valve of human heart is provided made of Polytetrafluoroethyene (PTFE) fabric material with a rectangular structure with one end to the septal surface and the other side to the anterior part of the said tricuspid valve and is of continuous structure and can be cut to produce a gap at the level of the septal leaflet and the distance between the two free ends can be set as per the discretion of the surgeon 2) The Annuloplasty ring as claimed in claim 1 for surgical procedures of tricuspid valve of human heart can assume varying sizes, based on the cuts the surgeon is making on the said septal part of the ring 3) The Annuloplasty ring as claimed in claim 1 is of rectangular in cross section and hence can make a 90 degree angle at the anterior septal junction of the tricuspid valve and septal posterior junction and on the other two angles of the ring for imparting desired shape and stability of the tricuspid valve thus enabling support to the septal leaflet for better cooptation. 4) The rectangular cross section of the annuplasty ring as claimed in claim 1, enables enhanced mobility of leaflets of tricuspid valve. 5) The Tricuspid valve of human heart is a curvilinear structure because of the flexibility of the two cut ends of the annuloplasty ring as claimed in claim 1, can conform to the shape of the anatomical tricuspid valve 6) The said Annuloplasty ring is made up of medically approved and compatible Polytetrafluoroethyene ( PTFE) material fabric which has long term durability properties 7) The structural arrangement of the said PTFE material is uniform and exhibits homogeneity throughout and hence facilitating uniform distribution of stress throughout the material 

) A method of supporting the tricuspid valve of the human heart by means of an Annuloplasty ring made of Polytetrafluoroethyene ( PTFE) material fabric, the said method comprising of a creating rectangular structure with one end to the said septal leaflet of the said tricuspid valve and the other side to the anterior and posterior leaflet of the said tricuspid valve and is of continuous structure and can be cut to produce a gap at the level of the septal leaflet and the distance between the two free ends can be set as per the discretion of the surgeon
